w:r.w:t: GitFlow Studio is an open-source, drag-and-drop, AI-powered Git client designed for the next generation of developers — particularly frontend, full-stack, and MNC-scale engineering teams who find existing Git tools intimidating, ugly, or insufficient for large codebases.

Like n8n transformed automation workflows into visual node graphs, GitFlow Studio transforms every Git operation — branching, merging, cherry-picking, conflict resolution, rebasing — into tactile, visual, AI-assisted actions on a beautiful canvas.
Git should feel like Figma, not a terminal. Every engineer — junior to senior — should have complete visibility, control, and confidence over their version history.
Frontend, Full-stack, and MNC Engineering Teams
Google Gemini API (Free Tier) + Groq (Free Tier)
React, React Flow, Tauri, Node.js, isomorphic-git
Every day, engineers across the world face the same Git frustrations:
The Git CLI is powerful but intimidating — one wrong command can destroy hours of work
Existing GUIs (GitKraken, SourceTree, Fork) are designed for individuals, not large teams
In MNC codebases with 500+ branches, there is zero visual structure or organization
Conflict resolution is a terrifying text-diff exercise with no guidance
Nobody knows why a commit was made three months ago
Junior engineers waste hours on Git basics that have nothing to do with their actual work
There is no AI layer in any current Git client — every suggestion has to be googled

Current Git tools fall into two camps: raw CLI power with no UX, or polished GUIs that are still fundamentally text-based. Nobody has built a truly visual, node-graph-based Git client with AI embedded at the core — especially one built for Gen-Z developers and enterprise-scale branch management.
The following is a comprehensive breakdown of all Git operations, sourced from official Git documentation, that GitFlow Studio must support visually and/or via AI assistance.
"New Repo" button → canvas spawns root commit node
"Open Remote" dialog → URL input → auto-renders graph
Settings panel → Profile tab with form fields
AI assistant sidebar — type any question
Live sidebar: Modified / Staged / Untracked file lists with color coding
Checkbox beside each file — check to stage. Drag files to Staged zone
Commit panel → AI suggests message → confirm → node appears on canvas
Right-click last commit node → "Amend" → edit in place
"Quick Commit" shortcut — stages and commits in one action
Split-pane diff viewer with syntax highlighting on file click
Right-click file → "Discard Changes" with undo option
Right-click file → "Stop Tracking" with confirmation dialog
Drag file in file tree → auto-generates git mv command
Click any commit node → "Add Note" → appears as tooltip
Right-click commit → "Reset to Here" → choose soft/mixed/hard
Left panel: Branch Explorer with folder grouping
Click "+" on canvas → name input → new node spawns
Remote branches shown in separate color in Branch Explorer
Click branch node → auto-checks out → canvas recenters
Click branch or right-click file → contextual action
Advanced panel: open multiple canvases for same repo
Drag one branch node onto another → merge confirmation → visual merge commit appears
Drag branch base to new commit → rebase animation plays
Visual rebase editor: drag to reorder, click to squash/drop/edit
Drag any commit bubble onto any branch → cherry-pick animation
Settings → Remotes tab → "+ Add Remote" button
"Fetch" button in toolbar → remote commits appear grayed on canvas
"Pull" button → shows what's incoming before applying
"Push" button → progress bar → remote badge updates on canvas
Gated behind confirmation with AI warning about destructive impact
Remotes panel with ping status indicator
Timeline view on canvas — scrollable, zoomable commit graph
Toggle between Detailed and Compact view in canvas toolbar
Default canvas view — this is the primary display mode
Click any commit node → detail panel slides in from right
File viewer → toggle Blame mode → inline author + date annotations
Bisect Wizard: mark good/bad commits → AI narrows it down
Shift-click two commit nodes → diff viewer opens automatically
"Time Machine" panel showing every state the repo was ever in
"Team Stats" view with per-author commit breakdown
"Stash" button → changes saved to Stash Drawer on left panel
Stash Drawer shows all saved stashes with timestamps
"Clear All" in stash drawer with confirmation
Tags shown as badge icons on commit nodes
"Add Tag" dialog → toggle Annotated → add message
File panel → "Clean Untracked" with preview before execution
Maintenance panel → "Check Integrity" with results report
Submodule panel: list, add, update, remove submodules visually
Advanced: check out only specific directories for large monorepos
4. UX Analysis — How Everything Should Work
Every Git operation must have a visual metaphor. No operation should require terminal knowledge.
AI is a co-pilot, not a crutch. It suggests, explains, and warns — the user always has final control.
Gen-Z first. The UI should feel closer to Figma or Linear than to SourceTree or GitKraken.
Enterprise-ready from day one. Branch folders, team chunking, and health indicators make it MNC-viable.

The canvas is the heart of GitFlow Studio. It renders the full git commit graph as an interactive, zoomable, draggable node graph built on React Flow. Every commit is a node. Every branch is a line. Every operation is a drag, click, or keyboard shortcut.
Clickable circle showing hash, author avatar, message preview. Click = detail panel. Right-click = context menu
Colored line connecting commits. Color is team/folder-based. Hover shows branch name tooltip
Pulsing ring around current checkout node
Gray ghost commits showing upstream state not yet merged locally
Pulsing red ring — immediately visible without searching
Small pill attached to commit node showing tag name
Dimmed opacity after 30 days of no activity
For MNC-scale repos with 500+ branches, the canvas supports a hierarchical folder system that groups branches by convention, team, owner, or ticket.

Each folder node on the canvas shows: branch count, conflict count, stale count, and health score. Folders can be collapsed/expanded, color-coded, and repositioned. Chunk Mode splits the canvas into team zones for org-wide visibility.
Conflict resolution is completely reimagined. Instead of a text diff editor, conflicts are presented as interactive cards:
Each conflict block is a collapsible card with Accept Ours / Accept Theirs / Edit buttons
AI analyzes both versions and suggests the winner with a confidence percentage and reasoning
Syntax highlighting matches the file language automatically
One-click "Accept All AI Suggestions" with ability to review each before confirming
After resolution, the merge commit node appears on canvas with a green checkmark
Push to remote with open PR
Since there is no budget for paid AI APIs, GitFlow Studio uses a layered free-tier strategy:
Model: gemini-2.5-flash — most generous free tier in the industry
Free quota: 500 requests/day with a Google account
Best for: Commit messages, conflict resolution, PR descriptions, risk analysis
Model: llama-3.3-70b-versatile — fastest free inference available
Free quota: Rate limited but ample for dev use
Best for: Real-time suggestions, branch naming, quick explanations
5.3 Fallback: Ollama (Fully Local, Zero API Cost)
Run LLMs locally — no internet, no API key, no cost ever
Best models: gemma2, phi4, mistral for code tasks
GitFlow Studio auto-detects Ollama if running on localhost:11434
GitFlow Studio manages free tier limits intelligently:
Cache AI responses for identical inputs (commit messages for same diff)
Batch low-priority requests (stale branch analysis runs once per day)
Route simple tasks to local rule-based logic (no API call needed)
Show daily API usage counter in Settings so users stay within limits
Allow users to bring their own API key for any provider
Branch Explorer, Stash Drawer, File Tree
Commit details, Diff viewer, AI suggestions
Current branch, sync status, AI activity indicator
Cmd+K — search any git action or branch
Dark mode is the default — light mode is an option, not the primary
Micro-animations on every action: merges flow, conflicts pulse, pushes shoot upward
No loading spinners — skeleton screens and progressive rendering only
Sound design option: subtle click sounds for commits, soft chime for successful push
Keyboard-first: every action accessible via Cmd+K command palette
Avatar-first: every commit, branch, and conflict shows the author's profile picture
Density toggle: Compact mode for power users, Spacious mode for learning
User saves files in their editor — GitFlow Studio detects changes via file watcher
Modified files appear in Left Sidebar with M (modified) / U (untracked) / D (deleted) badges
User checks files to stage OR clicks "Stage All"
Commit panel slides up from bottom — AI analyzes diff and suggests commit message in <2 seconds
User edits or accepts message → clicks Commit
New commit node animates onto canvas with author avatar and timestamp
User drags feature branch node onto main branch node on the canvas
Pre-merge analysis: AI scans for potential conflicts and shows risk score
Merge dialog: choose strategy (merge commit / squash / rebase)
If clean: merge commit node appears, branches visually connect
If conflicts: conflict nodes pulse red — user opens Conflict Resolver
AI resolves each conflict block with reasoning — user accepts/rejects per block
User clicks "Complete Merge" — merge commit finalizes, canvas updates
Auto-detection algorithm scans all branch names on load and applies these rules in order:
If branch starts with @: group under owner folder
If branch matches JIRA-NNNN/ or similar ticket pattern: group under ticket folder
If branch starts with feature/, bugfix/, hotfix/, release/: group by type
If branch starts with team-: group by team
If branch starts with service-: group by service
Everything else: placed in uncategorized, AI suggests folder assignment
n8n uses this — proven for node graphs at scale
Utility-first, dark mode native, Gen-Z design speed
Lightweight, cross-platform, native file system access
Pure JS Git — runs in browser and Node, no native deps
Most generous free tier — 500 req/day free
Fastest free LLM inference for real-time use
Zero cost, zero internet, privacy-first option
Local storage for settings, branch folders, AI cache
Sign in to push/pull to hosted remotes
Watches .git directory for changes, triggers canvas refresh
Executes all git operations — abstracted behind a unified API
Single source of truth: branches, commits, conflicts, folders, UI state
Transforms commit graph data into visual nodes and edges
Routes requests to Gemini / Groq / Ollama based on availability and quotas
Stores AI responses, branch folder config, user preferences
Manages push/pull/fetch operations with progress streaming
Tauri shell, React Flow canvas setup, isomorphic-git integration, open/clone repo
Commit graph rendering, branch lines, zoom/pan, commit detail panel
Stage files, commit with AI message suggestion (Gemini), switch branches
Create/delete/rename branches, branch folder auto-detection, folder UI
Drag-to-merge, 3-pane conflict resolver, AI conflict suggestions (Gemini)
Push/pull/fetch, GitHub OAuth, remote badge on canvas
Cherry-pick drag, stash drawer, interactive rebase, tag badges
Animations, command palette, dark mode, onboarding flow, GitHub release
Use the following master prompt to brief any AI coding assistant (Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot) on this project. It encapsulates the full product vision, stack, and constraints:
TYPE: Desktop app — visual, drag-and-drop, AI-powered Git client
STACK: React 18 + TypeScript, React Flow v11, Tauri, Tailwind CSS,
isomorphic-git, Zustand, Framer Motion, SQLite, Vitest
AI: Google Gemini Flash 2.5 (primary, free), Groq Llama 3.3 (secondary, free),
VISION: Like n8n turned automation into visual node graphs, GitFlow Studio
turns every Git operation into a visual, tactile, AI-assisted action.
Target: Gen-Z frontend/full-stack devs + MNC-scale teams.
CORE FEATURES TO BUILD (in priority order):
1. Interactive canvas: React Flow renders full git commit graph
2. Visual branch management: create/delete/rename by clicking on canvas
3. Drag-to-merge: drag branch node onto another to trigger merge
4. Drag cherry-pick: drag commit bubble to any branch
5. AI commit messages: Gemini analyzes staged diff, suggests message
6. AI conflict resolver: 3-pane card UI, AI picks winner with reasoning
7. Branch folder system: auto-group 500+ branches into collapsible folders
8. Chunk Mode: split canvas into team zones for MNC codebases
9. Stash drawer: visual stash management with apply/pop/branch
10. Command palette: Cmd+K for every git action
- Dark mode first (#0F172A background, #1E3A5F primary, #2563EB accent)
- Framer Motion for all transitions — nothing should be instant
- Conflict nodes pulse red, stale branches dim, HEAD pulses gently
- Inter font for UI, JetBrains Mono for code and hashes
- Every destructive action needs confirmation with AI risk warning
- Zero paid AI — use Gemini free tier (500/day) + Groq free tier only
- Cache all AI responses in SQLite to avoid repeat API calls
- isomorphic-git only — no shell exec, no native git binary required
- All data stays local — no telemetry, no cloud sync unless user opts in
- Open source (MIT license) — no paid features in v1
GitFlow Studio — Product Requirements Document v1.0
